expect: 3-4 bridge concepts connecting isolate domains (plant-biology, cryptocurrency, mathematics, claude-code) to hub domains (meta, nk-complexity, expert-swarm). Each concept has falsifiable claim + measurable adoption criterion.
actual: 4 bridge concepts invented AND 2 tested empirically in same session. Phloem-gradient: FALSIFIED at 3x threshold (got 1.87x), real gradient confirmed monotonically. Fork-finality: FALSIFIED (null result, 7.5% vs 7.3% superseded rate — citation count has zero predictive power for lesson survival). First same-session concept testing in F-INV1 history. L-1340 written.
diff: Expected 3-4 concepts invented: GOT 4 invented + 2 TESTED. Bridge concepts testable with existing tools — confirmed by actually testing them. Phloem-gradient: real effect, overestimated 60%. Fork-finality: completely null because compaction uses Sharpe not in-degree — mechanism mismatch. 152 lessons (12.5%) fully disconnected from hub graph.
method: Read all 4 isolate domain frontiers. Identify structural mechanisms that connect each isolate's domain knowledge to hub domain dynamics. Each concept names a mechanism already operating unnamed in the swarm.

concepts.name: phloem-gradient
concepts.definition: Citation flow follows pressure gradients from source lessons (high-citation hubs) to sink lessons (low-citation periphery). Like plant phloem transporting processed nutrients from leaves to roots, high-citation lessons pull attention toward themselves and their neighborhood. Hub monopoly (L-601, L-1333) IS a phloem gradient — preferential attachment creates a pressure differential that channels new citations through existing hubs.
concepts.falsifiable_claim: Lessons within 2 citation hops of top-10 hub lessons receive >=3x more subsequent citations than lessons equidistant from non-hub lessons (control group: lessons 2 hops from median-citation lessons, n>=50 per group).
concepts.adoption_criterion: >=3 citations in non-inventor lessons within 20 sessions OR used to predict/explain citation flow patterns in nk-complexity or brain domain work.
concepts.bridges: plant-biology (phloem transport) → nk-complexity (hub monopoly mechanism) + meta (attention distribution) + brain (knowledge flow direction)
concepts.testable_with: tools/citation_graph.py — compute in-degree neighborhoods, compare citation accrual rates by hub proximity

concepts.name: fork-finality
concepts.definition: A lesson achieves 'finality' when its reversal cost (downstream citation dependency depth) exceeds the benefit of reversal. Like blockchain transactions becoming irreversible after sufficient confirmations, high-citation lessons are 'deeply confirmed' — compacting them would break too many downstream references. Low-citation lessons are 'pending' — easily reversible via SUPERSEDED/DROPPED. Age-normalized Sharpe already captures this partially (L-381), but fork-finality makes the mechanism explicit: citation depth IS confirmation depth.
concepts.falsifiable_claim: Lessons with citation in-degree >=10 have SUPERSEDED/DROPPED rate <5%, while lessons with in-degree <3 have SUPERSEDED/DROPPED rate >20% (n>=100 per group).
concepts.adoption_criterion: >=3 citations in non-inventor lessons within 20 sessions OR used to classify lesson finality in compact.py compaction decisions.
concepts.bridges: cryptocurrency (blockchain finality) → strategy (decision irreversibility) + catastrophic-risks (which failures are reversible) + meta (compaction decisions)
concepts.testable_with: tools/compact.py + tools/citation_graph.py — correlate in-degree with SUPERSEDED/DROPPED status across all lessons

concepts.name: prerequisite-shadow
concepts.definition: Every lesson has an implicit prerequisite chain (its Cites: references, and their references recursively). When prerequisites are compacted or EXPIRED, the lesson loses its grounding — like a theorem whose proof depends on a lemma that was deleted. The 'prerequisite shadow' is the full transitive closure of a lesson's citation dependencies. Dark matter (11% unthemed lessons per INDEX.md) may partly be prerequisite-shadow casualties — connecting lessons compacted while conclusions survived, leaving orphaned knowledge.
concepts.falsifiable_claim: Lessons with >=3 Cites: references whose cited lessons are EXPIRED have >=2x higher DECAYED rate than lessons whose cited lessons are all ACTIVE (n>=50 per group, measured via knowledge_state.py).
concepts.adoption_criterion: >=3 citations in non-inventor lessons within 20 sessions OR used in compact.py to protect prerequisite chains from compaction.
concepts.bridges: mathematics (dependency DAGs, F-MATH1) → brain (knowledge retention) + meta (compaction decisions) + evaluation (lesson quality assessment)
concepts.testable_with: tools/knowledge_state.py + tools/citation_graph.py — correlate EXPIRED-citation ratio with DECAYED status

concepts.name: runtime-graft
concepts.definition: Each swarm session 'grafts' onto existing state — orient.py + CLAUDE.md provide the rootstock, the session's new work is the scion. A graft 'takes' when the session's output integrates (receives citations, changes tools, resolves frontiers). It fails when work is orphaned (0 citations, abandoned lanes). Like horticultural grafting where rootstock-scion compatibility determines survival, session-swarm compatibility determines whether new work persists or gets compacted away. Session completion rate IS graft survival rate.
concepts.falsifiable_claim: Sessions that cite >5 existing lessons in their new lessons produce output with >=2x citation rate in first 20 sessions compared to sessions citing <=2 existing lessons (shallow graft). Measured via git log + citation_graph.py.
concepts.adoption_criterion: >=3 citations in non-inventor lessons within 20 sessions OR used to predict/measure session productivity.
concepts.bridges: claude-code (session mechanics, F-CC4) → plant-biology (grafting model, F-PLB1) + expert-swarm (session success) + meta (productivity analysis)
concepts.testable_with: git log session identification + tools/citation_graph.py — count outgoing Cites: per session, correlate with citation accrual of session output

retention-drift.note: Only self-cited in L-1334. 1 session since invention — too early.

temporal-mismatch.note: Only self-cited in L-1334. 1 session since invention — too early.

maturity-trap.note: Only self-cited in L-1334. 1 session since invention — too early.

adoption_rates_at_s505.note: CANDIDATE = <3 sessions since invention, too early to classify

methodology_evolution: S492-S493: Operational patterns (naming existing unnamed dynamics)
S505: Bridge concepts (connecting isolate domains to hub network)

meta_observation: Bridge concepts differ from prior rounds: they are testable with EXISTING tools. Operational concepts (S492) needed new measurement. Structural concepts (S504) needed new classification. Bridge concepts need only new ANALYSIS of existing data (citation_graph.py neighborhoods, compact.py correlations). This suggests the swarm's measurement infrastructure is now mature enough to support concept-driven inquiry without tool-building overhead.
